findings.description: Every tier's exercise was generic ('pick a problem on a sticky note', 'pick a feature on your team's backlog'). Cohort is 7 people who all share the trinote2.0 codebase and one Trello board. Generic examples force every cohort member to do the same picking work.
findings.suggestedFix: Rewrite each tier exercise with explicit trinote2.0 grounding. Tier 1 lists three concrete script ideas. Tier 2 lists three real backlog candidates (saved searches, session detail print view, phone log dedupe). Tier 3 enumerates trinote's actual conventions (Flask blueprints, Vault, pytest layout). Tier 4 is a full walkthrough of building a bmad-trello-triage module on github.com/delorenj/mcp-server-trello.

findings.description: Tier 4 'Author' exercise was just '5 abstract steps to ship a module' with no concrete target. The capstone is supposed to be the moment the cohort feels BMAD click. Abstract instructions left them at a blank page.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a 5-step Trello-triage capstone: wire the MCP server, scaffold via /bmad-bmb-module, author the /triage-board workflow (pick + spec + move card), add a guard skill (refuse if WIP > 2), distribute to the team. Concrete tool calls named (list_boards, get_cards_by_list_id, move_card, add_comment).

findings.location: src/app/document.tsx, src/lib/progress.ts, src/app/pages/home.tsx, src/app/components/TierIndexClient.tsx, src/app/content/tiers.tsx (35 occurrences)
findings.description: User memory rule states 'No em dashes. Ever.' Em dashes leaked in across primer prose, page title, code comments, and a status placeholder.
findings.suggestedFix: Programmatic replace: 'X — Y' becomes 'X: Y'; ' — ' between sentences becomes '. Y' (capitalize); standalone em dash in placeholder becomes '...'.

notes: Adapted from ralph-wiggum-v2 game template; categories scoped to a small marketing/teaching site.
Hard requirements relaxed: TDD not enforced for content rewrites.
Verification harness: agent-browser, chrome-devtools, grep audit, vite build.
Cohort is 7 internal users sharing the trinote2.0 codebase. Iter 2 anchored every exercise to that shared context so the cohort runs the same exercises against the same repo.

Tier 4 capstone now produces a real, distributable artifact (bmad-trello-triage module) that uses the team's existing mcp-server-trello. The capstone is an actual deliverable for the team, not just a learning exercise.
